content:
{
  "title": "Program Sketch-Augmented Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Current code generation LLMs often produce incorrect or incomplete code, especially for complex tasks, due to insufficient intermediate reasoning in prompts. Existing prompting approaches do not explicitly guide the model through program structure before code synthesis.",
  "motivation": "Complex programming problems require high-level planning and decomposition, which LLMs struggle with when prompted naively. By integrating programmatic structure and intent into prompts, models may generate more accurate and functional code. This matters now as LLMs are increasingly used for real-world software development, where correctness and reliability are crucial.",
  "method": "We propose a two-step prompting method: first, the prompt requests the LLM to generate a brief program sketch—a structured, high-level outline of the solution in pseudocode or natural language steps. This sketch is then concatenated with the original problem description and fed as a prompt to the LLM for code generation. The approach leverages the model's planning abilities to scaffold subsequent code generation, encouraging better global coherence and correctness.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate on standard code generation benchmarks such as HumanEval and MBPP. Baselines include direct code generation prompts and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics are functional correctness (pass@k), syntactic correctness, and code similarity. We also conduct ablation studies to measure the effect of sketch quality and prompt formatting."
}
